issues.title: Inconsistent search param defaults across features
issues.description: Reports feature does not use .withDefault() on search params like audit and users features do. This means null values propagate into components instead of sensible defaults.
issues.fix: Add .withDefault(null) to parseAsString and parseAsFloat params, or establish a pattern: either all features use defaults or all use null. Currently: reports (no defaults), audit (uses .withDefault()), users (uses .withDefault()).

issues.title: Duplicate status enum definition
issues.description: ORDER_STATUS_VALUES array is defined directly in searchParams.ts as a runtime duplicate of the exported ORDER_STATUS_LIST constant from constants.ts. Creates two sources of truth.
issues.fix: Import ORDER_STATUS_LIST from constants.ts and use it directly: parseAsStringEnum<OrderStatus>(ORDER_STATUS_LIST)

issues.title: Missing Partial<ReportFilters> in function signature
issues.description: fetchReportOrders accepts full ReportFilters, but audit feature (similar pagination/filtering pattern) uses Partial<AuditFilters> to allow flexible filtering. Reports requires all fields to be passed.
issues.fix: Change signature to: fetchReportOrders(filters: Partial<ReportFilters>): Promise<ReportOrdersResponse> for consistency with audit feature pattern and flexibility.

issues.title: Missing stale time configuration in useQuery
issues.description: useReportOrders does not set staleTime, unlike useAuditLog which sets STALE_TIME_MS = 30_000. This means data is immediately stale and causes unnecessary refetches.
issues.fix: Add staleTime configuration to useQuery: const STALE_TIME_MS = 30_000; ... staleTime: STALE_TIME_MS

issues.title: Hardcoded filter reset object - good candidate for utility function
issues.description: handleClear() manually lists all 9 filter fields set to null. This is error-prone and duplicates the ReportFilters interface structure.
issues.fix: Create a utility: export function createEmptyFilters(): ReportFilters { return { dateFrom: null, dateTo: null, ... } }. Reuse in ReportFiltersBar and anywhere filters are reset.

issues.title: Good pattern: Status color mapping with fallback
issues.description: STATUS_COLOR_MAP uses Record<string, string> with ?? MUTED_STYLE fallback. This is safe and matches RoleBadge pattern in users feature. Excellent defensive programming.

issues.title: Good pattern: Type-safe translation keys with as Parameters<typeof t>[0]
issues.description: Uses 'as Parameters<typeof t>[0]' to safely cast dynamic translation key. Better than alternatives like any or string literals.

issues.title: Good pattern: XML escaping function for data safety
issues.description: escapeXml() properly escapes all 5 XML entities (&, <, >, ", '). This prevents XML injection attacks. Well-implemented security practice.

issues.title: Complex conditional logic in flatMap - candidate for extraction
issues.description: Lines 45-87 contain complex nested conditionals (if items.length === 0 vs map items). Logic is duplicated across both branches for order data, making it hard to maintain.
issues.fix: Extract into helper functions: function orderRowsForExport(order: ReportOrder): string[] { if (items.length === 0) {...} else {...} }. Improves readability and testability.

issues.title: Good pattern: Download helper function is reusable and well-isolated
issues.description: downloadExcel() is a pure function that doesn't depend on React or feature logic. Can be extracted to shared utilities and reused across features for consistency.
issues.fix: Consider moving downloadExcel() to shared/application/utils/ for reuse in users feature (which also exports data).

issues.title: Filter state synchronization complexity in handleFiltersChange
issues.description: Lines 24-31 use Object.fromEntries() and Object.entries() to ensure null values are properly set. This is fragile and hard to reason about.
issues.fix: Create a type-safe filter utility: function normalizeFilters(updates: Partial<ReportFilters>): ReportFilters { return { ...emptyFilters, ...updates } }

issues.title: Currency deduplication on every render
issues.description: Line 22: [...new Set(orders.map((o) => o.currency))].sort() runs on every render. Should be memoized to avoid recalculation.
issues.fix: Use useMemo: const currencies = useMemo(() => [...new Set(orders.map(o => o.currency))].sort(), [orders])

issues.title: Good pattern: Conditional render function for loading/error states
issues.description: renderContent() function clearly separates concerns: loading, error, success states. Makes template cleaner and logic easier to test.

issues.title: Good pattern: Client component clearly marked with 'use client'
issues.description: Proper Next.js 13+ App Router pattern. All presentation/page files correctly mark interactivity boundary.

issues.title: Minimal feature barrel export - missing opportunities
issues.description: Index file only exports ReportsPage. Other features (audit, users) export multiple utilities and components from their index files for easier imports.
issues.fix: Consider exporting public types, constants, hooks: export { ReportsPage, ORDER_STATUS_LIST, useReportOrders, type ReportOrder, type ReportFilters }

issues.title: Good pattern: Magic number extracted to named constant
issues.description: ORDER_ID_PREVIEW_LENGTH = 8 clearly documents intent. Better than hardcoded 8 in slice(0, 8).

issues.title: Missing key prop warning - item.id used but might not be unique
issues.description: Line 106 uses item.id as key in map(), but items within an order may not have globally unique IDs. Should use order.id + item index or compound key.
issues.fix: Use compound key: key={`${order.id}-${item.id}`} to ensure uniqueness across renders.

issues.title: Date formatting repeated - candidate for utility extraction
issues.description: new Date(order.created_at).toLocaleDateString() used in ReportTable and new Date(order.created_at).toLocaleString() in exportOrdersToExcel. Should be unified utility.
issues.fix: Create: export function formatOrderDate(date: string): string { return new Date(date).toLocaleDateString() }. Use everywhere.

issues.title: Good pattern: Semantic HTML with accessibility attributes
issues.description: Uses proper <label>, <input type='date'>, <select> with tid() attributes for testing. Follows web standards.

issues.title: No error handling in fetch - could mask network issues
issues.description: fetchReportOrders doesn't log or provide details on failed responses. Generic 'Failed to fetch report orders' hides root cause.
issues.fix: Add error details: throw new Error(`Failed to fetch report orders: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`)

patterns_found: Good: Status color mapping with fallback to MUTED_STYLE (PAT-006)
Good: Type-safe translation keys with Parameters<typeof t>[0] casting (PAT-007)
Good: XML escaping for data safety in exports (PAT-008)
Good: Download helper function is reusable and isolated (PAT-010)
Good: Conditional render function for state handling (PAT-013)
Good: Client component clearly marked with 'use client' (PAT-014)
Good: Magic number extracted to named constant (PAT-016)
Good: Semantic HTML with accessibility attributes (PAT-019)
Anti-pattern: Duplicate enum definitions (constants vs searchParams) (PAT-002)
Anti-pattern: Complex conditional logic in flatMap (PAT-009)
Inconsistency: Search params lack .withDefault() like other features (PAT-001)
Inconsistency: fetchReportOrders requires full ReportFilters vs Partial in audit (PAT-003)
Inconsistency: No staleTime set in useReportOrders (PAT-004)
Opportunity: Feature barrel export is minimal (PAT-015)
Risk: Missing error details in fetch (PAT-020)

rules_to_create.name: Consistent Search Parameter Defaults
rules_to_create.description: All feature search params should use .withDefault() to provide sensible defaults or explicitly use null across the codebase

rules_to_create.name: No Duplicate Enum Definitions
rules_to_create.description: Enum/constant lists should be defined once in constants.ts and imported everywhere else to maintain single source of truth

rules_to_create.name: API Functions Accept Partial Filter Types
rules_to_create.description: Infrastructure layer functions that accept filter objects should use Partial<T> to allow flexible querying

rules_to_create.name: useQuery Must Configure Stale Time
rules_to_create.description: All useQuery hooks should set staleTime to prevent unnecessary refetches. 30s is typical for data views.

rules_to_create.name: Export/Download Utilities Should Be in Shared
rules_to_create.description: Generic file download and export functions belong in shared/application/utils/ for reuse across features

rules_to_create.name: Feature Barrel Export Should Include Public API
rules_to_create.description: Feature index.ts should export key types, constants, and hooks for easier importing

similarities: Both use domain/types.ts, domain/constants.ts, domain/searchParams.ts pattern
Both use application/hooks pattern with useQuery
Both separate infrastructure queries from application hooks
Both use presentation/components and presentation/pages pattern
differences: Audit uses Partial<AuditFilters>, Reports uses full ReportFilters
Audit sets staleTime: 30_000, Reports does not
Audit uses .withDefault() in searchParams, Reports does not
Audit has more complex filtering logic (SQL safety checks)
Reports duplicates enum in searchParams vs importing from constants

similarities: Both have status/role badge components with color mapping
Both use same Clean Architecture layers
differences: Users has duplicate export files (export-csv.ts, exportCsv.ts) - potential cleanup opportunity
Users feature is much larger (more hooks, utils, components)
Reports focuses on table export, Users focuses on permission management

architectural_observations.clean_architecture_adherence: EXCELLENT - Clear separation: domain/types -> domain/constants -> domain/searchParams -> infrastructure -> application/hooks -> presentation/components/pages
architectural_observations.layering_consistency: GOOD - All features follow consistent layering, but could be stricter about Partial types in infrastructure
architectural_observations.type_safety: VERY_GOOD - Uses TypeScript union types, interfaces, and type-safe casting in translations
architectural_observations.error_handling: NEEDS_IMPROVEMENT - Missing error details in API fetch, generic error messages
architectural_observations.testing: NOT_VISIBLE_IN_REPORTS_FEATURE - No test files in reports (unlike audit, users, dashboard which have .test files)
architectural_observations.accessibility: GOOD - Proper semantic HTML, labels, testing IDs (tid), ARIA-friendly components

key_findings: Reports feature lacks test files (no .test.ts/.test.tsx) unlike all other features - recommend adding tests
Inconsistent search param defaults create friction compared to audit/users patterns - should standardize
Duplicate ORDER_STATUS_VALUES in searchParams.ts violates DRY principle - import from constants instead
Missing staleTime in useReportOrders causes unnecessary data refetches - add configuration
Complex filter reset logic is error-prone - create utility function for consistency
Date formatting logic is scattered - consolidate into shared utility
Feature barrel export is minimal - expose public API for better ergonomics
